title: Matrix Sum
description:
Time to crunch some numbers! You have a 3x4 grid (a 2D array) filled with integers. Your mission: add up every single element in the grid and print the total.

This is one of the most fundamental 2D array operations — traversing every element using nested loops. Think of it like scanning every cell in a spreadsheet and keeping a running total.
hints: Use a nested for loop — the outer loop goes through each row, and the inner loop goes through each column in that row.
Create an int variable called sum and set it to 0 before the loops. Inside the inner loop, add matrix[i][j] to sum.
After the nested loops, print the result with: System.out.println("Total sum: " + sum);

title: Transpose a Matrix
description:
Transposing a matrix means flipping it over its diagonal — rows become columns and columns become rows. Given a 3x3 matrix, create a brand-new 3x3 matrix that is the transpose of the original.

For example, the element at row 0, column 2 in the original should end up at row 2, column 0 in the transposed version. Print both the original and transposed matrices so you can see the flip in action!
hints: The key idea is: transposed[j][i] = matrix[i][j]. The row and column indices swap!
To print a row, loop through each element. Use if (j > 0) System.out.print(" ") before printing each value to get spaces between numbers, then System.out.println() after each row.
Use one nested loop to fill the transposed array, then two more nested loops to print the original and transposed matrices.

title: Tic-Tac-Toe Winner Checker
description:
You are building a tic-tac-toe judge! Given a 3x3 char array representing a game board, determine if there is a winner.

A player wins by getting three of their marks ('X' or 'O') in a row — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Your program should check all 8 possible winning combinations (3 rows, 3 columns, 2 diagonals) and print who wins. If nobody has three in a row, print "No winner".

The provided board has a sneaky diagonal win — can your code catch it?
hints: For each row, check if all three cells match and aren't spaces: board[i][0] == board[i][1] && board[i][1] == board[i][2] && board[i][0] != ' '
Columns work the same way but with a fixed column index: board[0][j] == board[1][j] && board[1][j] == board[2][j]. For diagonals, check positions (0,0)-(1,1)-(2,2) and (0,2)-(1,1)-(2,0).
After all checks, use: if (winner != ' ') System.out.println(winner + " wins!"); else System.out.println("No winner");

title: Find Largest in Each Row
description:
Every row has a champion! Given a 2D int array, your job is to find and print the largest value in each row.

This is a handy pattern that shows up everywhere — from finding the top scorer in each class to picking the brightest pixel in each row of an image. Loop through each row, track the maximum, and announce the winner!
expectedOutput:
Row 0 max: 8
Row 1 max: 9
Row 2 max: 11
hints: For each row, start by assuming the first element is the largest: int max = matrix[i][0];
Then loop through the rest of that row starting at index 1. If you find something bigger, update max.
After the inner loop finishes, print the result: System.out.println("Row " + i + " max: " + max);

title: Image Brightness Adjuster
description:
Digital images are just 2D arrays of numbers! Each value represents a pixel's brightness from 0 (black) to 255 (white). In this exercise, you will write a method that adjusts the brightness of every pixel by adding a value to it.

Here is the catch: brightness must stay within the valid range of 0 to 255. If adding the adjustment would push a pixel above 255, clamp it to 255. If it would drop below 0, clamp it to 0. This is exactly how real image editors work!

You will practice writing a separate method, passing 2D arrays as parameters, and using Math.min and Math.max for clamping.
hints: In adjustBrightness, loop through every element with nested for loops and add value to each pixel: pixels[i][j] += value; Then clamp it so it stays in range.
Use Math.min(255, Math.max(0, pixels[i][j])) to clamp a value between 0 and 255 in one clean line. Or combine it: pixels[i][j] = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, pixels[i][j] + value));
In printGrid, for each row use: if (j > 0) System.out.print(" "); System.out.print(grid[i][j]); then System.out.println(); after each row.
